paper.title: LQCDMaster: Agentic Scientific Computing for Lattice Quantum Chromodynamics Research

checks: The downloaded object is a readable PDF 1.7 document; pdfinfo reports 17 pages and 1875556 bytes.
The layout-preserving extraction is non-empty and readable from title and abstract through methods, data/code availability, both appendices, references, and the final manuscript page.
Sampled extraction sections preserve the 70-task result claims, workflow artifacts, generate_einsum boundary, skill table, data/code statement, DeepSeek appendix, and Claude Code comparison.
The immutable arXiv source archive is readable and contains seven members, including LQCDMaster.tex and the manuscript figures.
The arXiv API summary contains no withdrawal or retraction notice.

official_release.timing_boundary: The pinned commit predates the immutable arXiv v1 publication timestamp by about six hours and is the paper-linked acquisition target. This timing supports, but does not prove, exact paper-time implementation identity; review must verify paper/release conformance.
official_release.license_observation: README claims MIT and links LICENSE, but no LICENSE file is present at the pinned commit and the GitHub repository API reports no detected license.
acquisition_findings: The archive contains 8243 files (531963666 extracted bytes), including core architecture, prompts, five SKILL.md files, generate_einsum tooling, 70 benchmark task text files across the five paper classes, reference implementations/data, and extensive experiment artifacts.
The benchmark task counts match the README's 20 local 2pt, 10 nonlocal 2pt, 12 Wilson-loop, 13 meson 3pt, and 15 baryon 3pt split.

The experiments tree contains GPT-5.4 and DeepSeek benchmark records plus diagonal-Wilson-line and multi-hadron case-study artifacts; 169 submit_test_result.json files are present. Completeness and correspondence to reported denominators require deep review.

README reports 63 exact, three sign-flip, four failure outcomes for GPT-5.4 and 56 exact, two sign-flip, twelve failure outcomes for DeepSeek, while the paper appendix table reports three convention mismatches and eleven failures for DeepSeek. This is an acquisition-time discrepancy for review, not a resolved result claim.
No top-level LICENSE file is present despite the README's MIT badge and license link; do not assume redistribution terms are resolved.

acquisition_scope: Downloaded the real immutable arXiv v1 PDF and source archive, extracted layout-preserving full text, verified readable full-paper coverage, and pinned the paper-linked official repository at the queued commit. This is source acquisition and extraction verification, not a deep paper/code review, benchmark replay, result reproduction, endorsement, or evidence of expertise transfer, physical/scientific correctness, novelty, labor savings, professional validity, or readiness.
review_handoff: Use the immutable PDF/text/source and pinned repository archive. Audit Skill provenance and runtime adoption; all 70 task/reference/result chains; retries and complete denominators; exact/tolerance and convention classification; alternative implementation admissibility; configured-system identity; timing/cost evidence; case-study artifacts; and paper/release/result conformance. Resolve the DeepSeek count discrepancy and absent LICENSE before making release or reuse claims.
local_storage_note: The PDF, arXiv source archive, extracted source, repository archive, and extracted repository are local evidence and ignored by Git where large. The full-text extraction, metadata, GitHub metadata, inventory, paper-index entry, and this provenance record are intended for version control; re-fetch large artifacts from immutable URLs and verify SHA-256 when absent locally.
